description: Standard top-down TAM/SAM/SOM analysis for a SaaS product
prompt: I need a market sizing for a B2B SaaS tool that automates invoice processing for mid-market companies in North America. Can you do a TAM SAM SOM analysis?

expectations.description: Output contains a Market Sizing Summary table with TAM, SAM, and SOM rows
Output includes dollar values for all three tiers (TAM > SAM > SOM)
Output identifies top-down as the methodology used
Output includes a Key Assumptions section listing at least two numbered assumptions
Output includes a Data Sources section referencing at least one source
Output includes a Confidence Level assessment (High/Medium/Low)

description: User explicitly requests bottom-up approach with unit economics
prompt: Do a bottom-up market sizing for a mobile pet grooming service targeting urban areas in the US. I want to start from the number of pet owners and work up. We charge about $75 per grooming session.

expectations.description: Output uses bottom-up methodology starting from customer count and pricing
Output references the $75 per session price point provided by the user
Output includes step-by-step calculation building from unit economics to total market
Output quantifies the target customer base (pet owners in urban US areas)
Output does NOT contain placeholder values like $X.XB or [insert]

description: Market sizing that requires trend analysis with INC/CONST/DEC indicators
prompt: Size the market for AI-powered code review tools. I need growth trends and CAGR projections included. Is this market increasing or plateauing?

expectations.description: Output includes trend indicators using INC, CONST, or DEC terminology
Output includes CAGR percentage for at least one market tier
Output contains a summary table with Trend column
Output provides evidence supporting the trend assessment (e.g., analyst projections, adoption rates)
TAM, SAM, and SOM values are all present with dollar amounts

description: User requests scenario-based market sizing with bear/base/bull cases
prompt: I'm pitching to VCs next month. Can you do a market sizing for a direct-to-consumer plant-based protein snack brand? I need bear, base, and bull scenarios since this market is volatile.

expectations.description: Output includes a scenario table with Bear, Base, and Bull rows
Bear scenario TAM/SAM/SOM values are strictly lower than Bull scenario values
Output includes rationale explaining what drives each scenario (e.g., consumer adoption, competition)
Output contains a Market Sizing Summary section
Output does NOT use template placeholders

description: Market sizing using the value theory / willingness-to-pay methodology
prompt: Help me size the market for a cybersecurity tool that prevents data breaches. The average breach costs a company $4.5M. I want to approach this from the value we deliver, not just top-down industry numbers.

expectations.description: Output uses value theory approach referencing customer pain point cost
Output references the $4.5M breach cost figure provided by the user
Output calculates a capture rate (percentage of value the solution can charge)
Output still produces TAM/SAM/SOM figures even when using value theory
Output includes Key Assumptions documenting the capture rate and customer count assumptions

description: Edge case: user provides minimal context forcing the skill to ask clarifying questions or make stated assumptions

expectations.description: Output either asks clarifying questions about segment/geography/use-case OR explicitly states assumptions made to narrow the scope
If sizing is provided, it includes TAM/SAM/SOM structure rather than a single number
Output acknowledges the breadth of the drone market (commercial, consumer, military, etc.)
Output does NOT just return a single unsupported market size number without methodology

description: Edge case: user asks about a contracting/declining market to test DEC trend handling
prompt: Can you do a TAM/SAM/SOM for traditional print newspaper advertising in the United States? I know it's shrinking but I need the numbers for a comparison analysis.

expectations.description: Output identifies the market trend as declining or decreasing
Output includes negative or near-zero growth rates reflecting market contraction
Output still provides complete TAM/SAM/SOM structure even for declining market
Output notes risks or caveats about entering a declining market
Output includes Data Sources section
